mission.title: Install and Configure Kubernetes on Kubernetes
mission.description: Kubernetes (K8s) is an open-source system for managing containerized applications across multiple hosts, providing mechanisms for deployment, maintenance, and scaling. Installing Kubernetes allows you to orchestrate your containerized applications effectively in a production environment.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Helm repository for Kubernetes charts to your local Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add kvaps https://kvaps.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Kubernetes using Helm
steps.description:
Install Kubernetes using the Helm chart. This command will create a namespace called 'kube-system' if it doesn't already exist.
```bash
helm install kubernetes kvaps/kubernetes --namespace kube-system --create-namespace --version 0.13.5
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Kubernetes pods are running successfully in the 'kube-system' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kube-system
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Kubernetes deployment to ensure optimal performance.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment kubernetes --namespace kube-system --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Kubernetes Helm release from the 'kube-system' namespace.
```bash
helm uninstall kubernetes --namespace kube-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any custom resource definitions (CRDs) and other resources associated with the installation.
```bash
kubectl delete crd <crd-name>
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'kube-system' namespace to clean up any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kube-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Kubernetes deployment.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace kube-system -o yaml > kube-system-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to fetch the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes installation
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Kubernetes Helm release to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade kubernetes kvaps/kubernetes --namespace kube-system --version 0.13.5
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the upgraded Kubernetes pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace kube-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your pods are stuck in the Pending state, check for resource availability.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace kube-system
``` 
Ensure that there are sufficient resources available in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Helm install fails with 'Chart not found'
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter a 'Chart not found' error, ensure that the Helm repository is added correctly.
```bash
helm repo list
``` 
If the repository is missing, re-add it using the add command.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the Kubernetes service is not reachable, check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace kube-system
``` 
Ensure that the service is running and the correct ports are exposed.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Kubernetes will result in all pods running in the 'kube-system' namespace and the ability to manage your containerized applications effectively. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods and services.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add kvaps https://kvaps.github.io/charts
helm repo update
kubectl set resources deployment kubernetes --namespace kube-system --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a working Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl tools installed.
